channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Windsurf Cascade uses AI to help beginners code games and applications
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. I don’t know how to code.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: AI, power availability and Intel’s future
item.description: As 2025 kicks off, it’s time to dust off the crystal ball. Here are my data center predictions for the coming year. 1) Trump backs off on tech tariffs For all the handwringing, Trump has a history of not following through on his threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fireworks mortars, fuel canisters found in Cybertruck that exploded outside Trump hotel in Las Vegas
item.description: A Cybertruck explosion outside of the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas was caused by firework mortars and camp fuel canisters, according to investigators. The explosion killed one person inside the vehicle and injured at least seven others nearby.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: I'm raising my bread-making game in 2025 with these 5 affordable gadgets and apps
item.description: These handy kitchen gadgets will help you become the ultimate bread baker in 2025 – without using a bread maker.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Honkai: Star Rail Version 3.0 Arrives on January 15 - A New Chapter Unfolds
item.description: Get ready for a massive update in Honkai: Star Rail! Explore the new world of Amphoreus, encounter a mysterious companion named Mem, and experience two new powerful five-star playable characters.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: Everything new on Max in January 2025
item.description: It's time to get cosy this January with a whole new slate of Max movies to watch and Max TV shows to binge.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tractor Supply’s Rob Mills on the CIO as chief growth officer
item.description: Rob Mills has been instrumental in driving Tractor Supply Co.’s rapid growth, profitability, and world-class customer experience. Mills joined Tractor Supply a decade ago as its CIO, and within four years, his role, influence, and impact had expanded significantly.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Fine-tuning Azure OpenAI models in Azure AI Foundry
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) like the OpenAI models used by Azure are general-purpose tools for building many different types of generative AI-powered applications, from chatbots to agent-powered workflows.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck explodes in front of Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: A Tesla Cybertruck exploded in front of President-elect Trump’s Las Vegas hotel Wednesday, killing one person and injuring seven others. Las Vegas Metropolitan Police first received a report of an explosion and fire at Trump International Hotel at around 8:40 a.m. local time.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Inside Out 2 director has ‘lots of ideas for other lands and emotions in the mind’ for a third movie
item.description: TechRadar speaks to the writer and director of Pixar’s Inside Out 2, Kelsey Mann, about the potential for a third movie.
item.dc:creator: amelia.schwanke@futurenet.com (Amelia Schwanke), TechRadar

item.title: Rogue Two
item.description: Don Parente, the vice president of sales and solution architecture for MetTel Federal, provides recommendations for agencies to better manage space IT. The post Rogue Two — Your IT sprawl is about to expand to space first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Revealed: The 10 most popular and worst passwords of 2024
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson cites a new report that says many people still use weak passwords like "123456," risking their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s Supreme Court on Monday issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hackers have posted some RIBridges data on the dark web, McKee says
item.description: The state disclosed earlier this month that cybercriminals had gained access to the state's IT system for social services.
item.dc:creator: Ted Nesi, NewsNation

item.title: The future of open source will be messy
item.description: Open Source Initiative (OSI) chief Stefano Maffulli says Meta is “bullying” the industry on the concept of open source. The Economist more colorfully characterizes the dispute as “bare-it-all purists … confronting Meta, the social-media giant controlled by a mankini-clad Mark Zuckerberg.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: These glasses let you 'see' conversations in real time
item.description: When I heard about AI glasses that let you "see" what people are saying, I was skeptical. But after trying them on, I was proven wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, NewsNation

item.title: Are your smart home cameras spying on you? Study reveals shocking data grabs
item.description: A new study says outdoor security camera apps are among the biggest collectors of user data, including phone numbers, payment details and precise location.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump weighs in on H-1B debate, backs immigration visas
item.description: “I’ve always liked the visas, I have always been in favor of the visas. That’s why we have them,” Trump said in a phone interview with The New York Post published Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico tests cellphone app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico is developing a cellphone app that will allow migrants to warn relatives and local consulates if they think they are about to be detained by the U.S. immigration department, a senior official said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
